opp.ais_why:
• The NextStep's explicit next-step plan reads: 'Schedule a 90-min S2C overview demo (focus: iSource, iContract, iSupplier, iRisk, Merlin Intake) with Efficio + client stakeholders; share Oracle EBS vs modern S2C capability-gap matrix.' Merlin Intake is named as a demo focus ‚Äî but as a rep-positioned module alongside traditional S2C, not as a buyer-stated requirement.
• On 13 Apr 2026, the rep note records the project lead: 'watch this space, decision on RFP pending.' On 15 Mar 2026: 'RFI submitted.' On 8 Oct 2025: 'Met Andre, Errol & two Efficio guys in PIF Tower... they will be running a formal exercise for them only (i.e. not the PIF companies) in 2026 - after Eid (end March) at the very earliest - will require KSA hosting.' The buyer-side conversation centres on KSA hosting requirements and Efficio's vendor-agnostic posture ‚Äî not AI capability.
• Confidence is Medium. PIF has a large AI product loadout (Merlin + Merlin Intake) and an active Efficio-led RFI process, but no buyer quote names AI as evaluation criterion. The rep-positioned Merlin Intake focus is the only AI signal; the deal is more advanced on hosting/Efficio politics than on AI agenda. Score holds at 5 (boundary DOWN per rubric).

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or financial impact has been articulated by the relevant stakeholder 8 Oct 2025 meeting noted PIF will run a 'formal exercise for them only' covering 'whole S2P market' after Eid 2026, but no specific pain points, cost savings targets, cycle-time reduction, headcount efficiency, or ROI metrics were captured (SF Next_Step__c, 8 Oct 2025). No Avoma calls exist to provide buyer-stated pain. The absence of a quantified value case 28 days from close with no Economic Buyer engagement means there is no financial anchor to justify the $200k investment or to build C-level sponsorship. Without metrics, the deal has no compelling event and no basis for urgency. The highest-leverage move is to secure an Economic Buyer meeting and extract the business case: what operational or financial problem does PIF's S2P function solve, what is the cost of inaction, and what ROI does PIF expect from modernizing their procurement stack.

sources: No Avoma calls with PIF stakeholders
champion.narrative: Zycus has no champion inside PIF. All buyer access is mediated by the relevant stakeholder, specifically Andrew Waterhouse (project lead, Efficio). Andrew has been responsive but passive: he confirmed on 13 May 2026 'no update on RFP yet' and on 2 Jul 2026 (via the deal owner's outreach) 'nothing required from Zycus team at this stage' (SF Next_Step__c, 13 May 2026, 2 Jul 2026). Andrew is not an advocate; he is a gatekeeper executing a vendor-agnostic RFP process. When Zycus Alliances attempted to influence Efficio on 20 Mar 2026, Efficio responded 'not interested, Efficio are vendor agnostic' (SF Next_Step__c, 20 Mar 2026). Inside PIF, the Contact Roles list four individuals (Sofian Ouahchi, Abdullah Almanyah, Lujain Mosaeb, Supplier Relationship Management generic role), but none have been engaged on an Avoma call or shown advocacy. The Economic Buyer (Abdulaziz Alsuwailem, SVP) is identified but unengaged. The lack of a champion 28 days from close in a six-vendor bake-off is fatal: Zycus has no one inside PIF pushing for Zycus, no visibility into the scoring or deliberations, and no political leverage. Competitors with direct PIF relationships will have a structural advantage. The immediate move is twofold: (1) secure the EB meeting and convert Alsuwailem or a direct report into an active sponsor, and (2) identify a coach inside PIF who can provide intelligence on the RFP status and competitive positioning. Without a champion, the deal is passive and reactive.

competition.narrative: PIF's RFP includes six named vendors: SAP Ariba, GEP Worldwide, Coupa, iValua, JAGGAER, and 'Others' (SF Competitors__c). SAP Ariba is the primary competitor given their dominant Middle East presence, existing KSA hosting infrastructure, and deep penetration in sovereign and enterprise accounts across the Gulf. GEP Worldwide has a strong Middle East consulting and software footprint. The 8 Oct 2025 meeting noted the RFP will 'involve whole S2P market', confirming this is a full bake-off, not a sole-source or shortlist-first process (SF Next_Step__c, 8 Oct 2025). However, Zycus has no intelligence on which vendors are advancing, how PIF is scoring the RFI responses, or whether any competitor has secured champion or EB access. Efficio's vendor-agnostic stance means they will not favor Zycus. The wedge: Zycus must position Merlin (AI-driven intake, negotiation agent, supplier intelligence) as differentiated vs Ariba's legacy workflow automation, and must anchor the story on PIF's strategic AI and digital-transformation mandate. However, without a champion to carry this narrative internally and without EB engagement to validate the AI appetite, the wedge is theoretical. The move is to (1) secure a competitive review debrief when the EB meeting occurs, (2) confirm whether PIF has a strategic AI procurement initiative that Merlin can anchor to, and (3) surface any Ariba/GEP/Coupa gaps (e.g. KSA hosting latency, AI maturity, Oracle EBS integration complexity) that Zycus can exploit.

sources: Inferred from Oracle EBS reference in Next_Step__c
identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated by the relevant stakeholder 10 Sep 2025 outreach from Efficio referenced a 'high-level KSA opportunity in 2026' for PIF's own S2P function (not their portfolio companies), implying PIF's procurement organization is modernizing or consolidating their stack, but no pain owner, cost driver, cycle-time bottleneck, or compliance risk was named (SF Next_Step__c, 10 Sep 2025). The 8 Oct 2025 meeting noted PIF will run a formal exercise 'after Eid (end March) at the very earliest' and will 'involve whole S2P market', suggesting a strategic initiative rather than a crisis-driven replacement, but again no quantified pain (SF Next_Step__c, 8 Oct 2025). Inferred pain: PIF likely has fragmented or legacy procurement systems (possibly Oracle EBS, given the Next_Step__c action to 'share Oracle EBS vs modern S2C capability-gap matrix'), manual processes, and a desire for digital transformation and AI-enabled procurement. However, this is inference, not confirmed pain. Without a named pain owner (e.g. the SVP who owns procurement efficiency, the CFO who owns cost reduction) and without a stated cost of inaction, the deal has no urgency. The move is to use the EB meeting to extract the pain: what is broken today, what is the financial impact, and who owns the problem.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, or InfoSec process have been discussed. Early in the cycle (22 Jan 2026) PIF requested further info and Zycus liaised with Legal as a 'precursor to the formal RFI', and on 12 Jan 2026 an NDA, Code of Conduct, and information request were submitted (SF Next_Step__c, 22 Jan 2026, 12 Jan 2026). However, no MSA, SOW, redlining, or ERP integration scoping has occurred. Efficio is the process mediator, but their role in contracting (vs evaluation only) is undefined. PIF is a sovereign wealth fund, implying government procurement rules, legal review by the relevant stakeholder, and likely InfoSec and data-residency audits (given the KSA hosting requirement). With 28 days to close and no commercials, no pricing, and no EB engagement, the paper process is entirely unmapped. The normal SOW/MSA cycle for a $200k enterprise S2P deal is 30-45 days AFTER commercial agreement. The move is to surface the contracting and legal timeline in the EB meeting and to confirm whether PIF's procurement rules allow a 28-day contracting cycle or if the close date must slip.

economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is Abdulaziz Alsuwailem, SVP at PIF, identified 18 Jun 2026 in Next_Step__c when the deal owner drafted a BD intro email to 'setup new meeting with Abdulaziz Alsuwailem (SVP / Economic Buyer) and including all team from PIF' (SF Next_Step__c, 18 Jun 2026). However, as of 2 Jul 2026, there is no confirmation that this meeting occurred or that Alsuwailem has engaged. The 2 Jul Next_Step__c entry states the deal owner will 'follow up email with Andrew Waterhouse for meeting at 2nd week of July as he mentioned nothing required from Zycus team at this stage', indicating the BD intro has not yet yielded EB engagement (SF Next_Step__c, 2 Jul 2026). Alsuwailem has never appeared in Contact Roles, Tasks, or Avoma calls. Without direct EB engagement 28 days from close, budget authority, C-level sponsorship, approval chain, and commercial sign-off are unmapped. The EB's absence is fatal: no sovereign-wealth-fund $200k S2P deal closes without the SVP who controls the budget. The immediate move is to escalate through the deal owner's manager to secure the EB meeting this week.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 10 Sep 2025, 2 Mar 2026, 15 Mar 2026, 13 Apr 2026, 2 Jul 2026
decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal multi-vendor RFI/RFP process mediated by the relevant stakeholder, a vendor-agnostic advisory firm. The timeline was: Efficio reached out 10 Sep 2025, RFI received 2 Mar 2026 (early), submitted 15 Mar 2026, and PIF was expected to issue the RFP decision after Eid (late Mar/Apr 2026) (SF Next_Step__c, 10 Sep 2025, 2 Mar 2026, 15 Mar 2026). However, as of 13 Apr 2026 the project lead (Andrew Waterhouse, Efficio) said 'watch this space, decision on RFP pending', and 81 days later (2 Jul 2026) there is still no update (SF Next_Step__c, 13 Apr 2026, 2 Jul 2026). The approval chain is unknown: Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, and Multiple_approvals__c were not queried, and no Avoma calls exist to map the internal sign-off flow. Contact Roles show only mid-level stakeholders (Senior Digital Consultant, Acting Head of Communications & Marketing Category) with no C-level or CFO representation. The process has stalled at the RFP-decision gate for 81 days with no visibility into why. The deal is blind: Zycus does not know if PIF is still evaluating, if the timeline slipped, if a competitor has advanced, or if the project is on hold. The move is to escalate through the EB to restart the process and demand transparency on the shortlist timeline.

sources: No Avoma calls with PIF stakeholders
decision_criteria.narrative: PIF's decision criteria are inferred from the RFI/RFP process structure and the 8 Oct 2025 scoping discussion, but have never been explicitly validated with the buyer. The 8 Oct 2025 meeting noted the formal exercise will 'involve whole S2P market' and 'will require KSA hosting', implying criteria include: (1) full S2P suite coverage (source-to-contract and procure-to-pay), (2) KSA data residency and local hosting, (3) regulatory compliance for a Saudi sovereign entity, and (4) competitive positioning against the named vendors (Ariba, GEP, Coupa, iValua, JAGGAER) (SF Next_Step__c, 8 Oct 2025). The RFI was submitted 15 Mar 2026, suggesting PIF structured their requirements in a formal evaluation framework, but Zycus has never seen the scoring matrix, weighting, or decision timeline (SF Next_Step__c, 15 Mar 2026). No use-case validation, test-case session, or workshop has occurred to confirm fit. Without direct EB or Decision Maker dialogue, Zycus is guessing at the criteria. The move is to request a debrief on the RFI scoring and a live criteria review session when the BD intro to the EB is secured.

flags: Partner-mediated access is a control vulnerability. All buyer access runs through Efficio (Sofian Ouahchi), with no direct Zycus-to-PIF relationship. The 18 Jun outreach via partner BD to reconnect with the PIF Economic Buyer is the first attempt to go direct. In a vendor-agnostic partner-led RFP, Zycus needs a direct champion inside PIF who can share RFP feedback, navigate approvals, and advocate internally. The EB meeting (if confirmed) is the opportunity to identify and begin developing that champion. Without it, Zycus is competing blind and will lose if a competitor builds direct PIF relationships.

No RFP feedback or shortlist visibility 103 days post-submission. The RFI was submitted 15 Mar 2026, and the project lead's 13 Apr response ('decision pending') is the last update. In a multi-vendor bake-off against SAP Ariba, GEP, Coupa, iValua, and JAGGAER, not knowing where Zycus ranks or what gaps the RFP scoring surfaced is a critical blind spot. The immediate move is to request a debrief from Efficio or the project lead: 'What did we do well in the RFI? Where did we fall short? Are we in the shortlist?' Use that feedback to tailor the EB meeting and any follow-on demo to address PIF's specific concerns.

Merlin AI is in scope but not yet positioned as the wedge. The product scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake, which are Zycus's AI-driven sourcing, contract intelligence, and intake-orchestration differentiators in a field of legacy and cloud-native peers. In a sovereign-wealth-fund S2P transformation, agentic AI that automates sourcing-event setup, contract-risk analysis, and supplier-negotiation workflows is a high-value wedge. The 90-min S2C demo listed in Next_Step__c includes Merlin Intake but does not emphasize the AI narrative. The next move is to make Merlin the centerpiece of the EB meeting: show a live Merlin negotiation agent on supplier price history, demonstrate Merlin Intake's ability to orchestrate complex multi-stakeholder requests, and tie it to PIF's transformation ROI. This is the story that beats Ariba and GEP.

PIF SVP (Economic Buyer, role referenced in Next_Step__c)

Economic Buyer (PIF SVP) has not been engaged directly by the relevant stakeholder requested 18 Jun 2026 via partner BD intro, not yet confirmed as of 26 Jun. Without EB engagement, budget authority, C-level sponsorship, and approval-chain mapping are unknown. In a $200k sovereign-wealth-fund deal 35 days from close, the absence of EB alignment is a critical vulnerability.

Partner-mediated access is a control vulnerability. All buyer access runs through Efficio (Sofian Ouahchi), with no direct Zycus-to-PIF relationship. The 18 Jun outreach via partner BD to reconnect with the PIF Economic Buyer is the first attempt to go direct. In a vendor-agnostic partner-led RFP, Zycus needs a direct champion inside PIF who can share RFP feedback, navigate approvals, and advocate internally. The EB meeting (if confirmed) is the opportunity to identify and begin developing that champion. Without it, Zycus is competing blind and will lose if a competitor builds direct PIF relationships.

No RFP feedback or shortlist visibility 103 days post-submission. The RFI was submitted 15 Mar 2026, and the project lead's 13 Apr response ('decision pending') is the last update. In a multi-vendor bake-off against SAP Ariba, GEP, Coupa, iValua, and JAGGAER, not knowing where Zycus ranks or what gaps the RFP scoring surfaced is a critical blind spot. The immediate move is to request a debrief from Efficio or the project lead: 'What did we do well in the RFI? Where did we fall short? Are we in the shortlist?' Use that feedback to tailor the EB meeting and any follow-on demo to address PIF's specific concerns.

Merlin AI is in scope but not yet positioned as the wedge. The product scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake, which are Zycus's AI-driven sourcing, contract intelligence, and intake-orchestration differentiators in a field of legacy and cloud-native peers. In a sovereign-wealth-fund S2P transformation, agentic AI that automates sourcing-event setup, contract-risk analysis, and supplier-negotiation workflows is a high-value wedge. The 90-min S2C demo listed in Next_Step__c includes Merlin Intake but does not emphasize the AI narrative. The next move is to make Merlin the centerpiece of the EB meeting: show a live Merlin negotiation agent on supplier price history, demonstrate Merlin Intake's ability to orchestrate complex multi-stakeholder requests, and tie it to PIF's transformation ROI. This is the story that beats Ariba and GEP.

The EB meeting (if confirmed) is the opportunity to identify and begin developing that champion. Without it, Zycus is competing blind and will lose if a competitor builds direct PIF relationships.

The immediate move is to request a debrief from Efficio or the project lead: 'What did we do well in the RFI? Where did we fall short? Are we in the shortlist?' Use that feedback to tailor the EB meeting and any follow-on demo to address PIF's specific concerns.

value.flag: Merlin AI is in scope but not yet positioned as the wedge. The product scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake, which are Zycus's AI-driven sourcing, contract intelligence, and intake-orchestration differentiators in a field of legacy and cloud-native peers. In a sovereign-wealth-fund S2P transformation, agentic AI that automates sourcing-event setup, contract-risk analysis, and supplier-negotiation workflows is a high-value wedge. The 90-min S2C demo listed in Next_Step__c includes Merlin Intake but does not emphasize the AI narrative. The next move is to make Merlin the centerpiece of the EB meeting: show a live Merlin negotiation agent on supplier price history, demonstrate Merlin Intake's ability to orchestrate complex multi-stakeholder requests, and tie it to PIF's transformation ROI. This is the story that beats Ariba and GEP.
